title: Define CrowdAnki-aligned card/deck data model + provenance/attribution + GUID rules
context: All deck authoring, build tooling, and CI gates depend on a well-defined, stable data model. CrowdAnki JSON is the interchange format, but it needs explicit extensions for per-card provenance, attribution, SPDX license fields, and deterministic GUID derivation rules. This model is the contract between card authors, build tooling, linters, and the publishing pipeline.
objective: Define and publish the CrowdAnki-aligned card/deck data model schema, including required provenance and attribution fields, SPDX license fields, and deterministic GUID derivation rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines the JSON schema for the deck and card data model, compatible with CrowdAnki's format.
Specifies required per-card fields: source URL, SPDX-style license identifier, attribution string, and retrieval/authoring date.
Defines deterministic GUID derivation rules (e.g., from deck id + note type + stable key) that are collision-free and stable across updates.
Specifies which CrowdAnki fields are required, optional, or forbidden (e.g., no baked-in scheduling state).
Documents the allowed note types (Basic, Basic-and-reversed, Cloze) and their field structure.
Schema is versioned and published as a JSON Schema file with accompanying documentation, CC-BY-4.0.
output: A versioned JSON Schema file (schema/card-model.schema.json) and accompanying documentation (docs/data-model.md) defining the card/deck data model, provenance fields, and GUID rules, CC-BY-4.0.
